message.content:
Run CODEX (GPT-5.6) to gate NEEDS-ALIAS renames. Write /tmp/alias_gate.md then 'cd /tmp && timeout 240 codex exec --skip-git-repo-check - < /tmp/alias_gate.md 2>&1 | tail -30' (embed evidence inline; medium effort; no sandbox flags; bubblewrap warning fine).
Context: renamed control-surface symbols while adding backward-compat aliases so old strings/monkeypatch-targets/env-vars still work. Result: {"applied":15,"skipped":["row 15 (GEMINI-ARCH structured_data tag): SOURCE-GREP harness assertion — anti_tunnel_view_test.py:172/583 asserts the exact literal 'GEMINI-ARCH: ACCUMULATE structured_data' appears in graph.py source text (no fallback at :583); an alias cannot satisfy a source-text grep. Needs human test update.","row 31 (slice_005_beat_both_benchmark): persisted-string VALUE asserted by exact equality in tests (test_benchmark_route.py:53, test_app_slice_route_mount.py:275 'assert body["slice"] == "slice_005_beat_both_benchmark"') and must match benchmark_route.py:141 producer; an inline positional string with no symbol cannot be aliased while keeping the exact-value assertions green.","row 99 (rubber_stamp_suspect): inline string rule-label passed positionally to _make_anomaly; test_live_monitor.py:198 asserts 'rubber_stamp_suspect' in rules by exact value. No symbol to alias; renaming the literal breaks the exact-value assertion, keeping it is a no-op.","row 100 (token_explosion): same as row 99 — inline string rule-label; test_live_monitor.py:289 asserts a["rule"]=='token_explosion' by exact value. No aliasable symbol.","row 105 (PG_V2_ENABLED): env-var control-surface string (inline os.getenv, no separable symbol) PLUS source-grep assertion test_b102_graph_v4.py:212 'assert "PG_V2_ENABLED" in source'. Renaming breaks operator settings and the source grep; per rule keep env string.","row 140 (PG_JUNK_SOURCE_SCREEN): env-var control-surface string (inline os.getenv) with monkeypatch.setenv/delenv on the old string in test_u20_junk_span_screen.py:154/169. Keep env string per rule; no separable symbol to rename.","row 146 (content_integrity_junk): cross-module persisted dict-KEY read by exact name in junk_deletion_gate.py, build_bank.py (comment: 'reads these exact names'), and 3 tests; the line-15688 usage is a reader .get(). Aliasing one reader cannot safely change the multi-module/persisted contract.","row 148 (junk_deletion_gate module import in run_honest_sweep): CONFLICT — worklist gives 3 different new module names for the same file (row 148 nonsource_deletion_gate, row 239 content_integrity_deletion_gate, row 242 off_topic_deletion_gate). Canonical name unresolved.","row 160 (_WINNER_SLATE_ON_PAID_PATH_ENV): AST/source-grep harness — test_wave_b_ws2_paid_slate.py extracts only nodes whose def-name is in _WANTED (old names) and execs them in isolation; an alias node references a non-extracted new name (NameError). Alias cannot satisfy the AST-name harness.","row 161 (_PAID_PATH_WINNER_FLAGS): same AST-extraction harness — test asserts ns['_PAID_PATH_WINNER_FLAGS'] against the isolated exec namespace keyed on the old name; alias dangles.","row 162 (winner_slate_on_paid_path_enabled): same AST-extraction harness (_WANTED old name) — alias not resolvable in the isolated exec.","row 163 (apply_winner_slate_on_paid_path): AST call-graph source assertion — operational_readiness_preflight._source_calls(src,'main_async','apply_winner_slate_on_paid_path') requires the exact old name to appear as a call inside main_async; alias cannot satisfy it.","row 165 (PG_S15_CORROBORATED_HONEST_LABEL): env-var control-surface string (inline os.environ.get) + config-key-list membership asserted in test_prelaunch_hardening_ideepfix001.py and run_gate_b.py env dicts on the old string. Keep env string per rule.","row 168 (PG_CONTRADICTION_RENDER_HONEST): env-var control-surface string (inline os.environ.get) + monkeypatch.setenv/delenv on the old string across test_deepfix_b4render_9_13_16_22.py + run_gate_b.py config lists. Keep env string per rule.","row 178 (token_honesty manifest key): persisted manifest-field KEY written under an explicit byte-identical-manifest contract (surrounding comment); old key already works untouched. Adding an alias key risks the acceptance oracle/manifest golden — a contract-version bump is a human decision.","row 184 (BEAT_BOTH_SCORERS): worklist explicitly states 'rename deferred, no target'. Nothing to apply.","row 190 (S1V1_..._RUN_V1): worklist '(no target; persisted vector_id)' — a persisted-string VALUE written into run artifacts with no target name. Nothing to apply.","row 217 (HonestSweepJobRunner re-export): CONFLICT — re-export target SweepJobRunner (row 217) disagrees with definition target V30SweepJobRunner (row 221). Canonical name unresolved.","row 218 (HonestSweepJobRunnerConfig): entangled in the same public re-export cluster whose canonical prefix is unresolved by the 217-vs-221 / 219-vs-222 conflicts; renaming Config alone (SweepJobRunnerConfig) while the sibling Runner stays unresolved would create an inconsistent public surface.","row 219 (make_default_honest_sweep_job_runner re-export): CONFLICT — re-export target make_default_sweep_job_runner (row 219) disagrees with definition target make_default_v30_sweep_job_runner (row 222). Canonical name unresolved.","row 221 (HonestSweepJobRunner definition): CONFLICT — definition target V30SweepJobRunner disagrees with re-export target SweepJobRunner (row 217). Unresolved.","row 222 (make_default_honest_sweep_job_runner definition): CONFLICT — definition target make_default_v30_sweep_job_runner disagrees with re-export target make_default_sweep_job_runner (row 219). Unresolved.","row 224 (BEAT-BOTH verdict tag): persisted verdict-literal VALUES ('BEAT-BOTH'|'BEAT-ONE'|'TIE' in manifests) + exact-substring test assertion test_report_renderer.py:93 'assert "POLARIS BEAT-BOTH" in md'. Alias cannot satisfy the persisted-value + source assertion.","row 232 (POLARIS BEAT-BOTH title): persisted rendered-string VALUE asserted by exact substring 'assert "POLARIS BEAT-BOTH" in md' (test_report_renderer.py:93). Alias cannot satisfy an exact-output assertion.","row 238 (is_row_content_junk -> is_row_content_low_quality): CONFLICT — rows 238 and 240 rename the SAME function is_row_content_junk to two different names (low_quality vs integrity_violation). Canonical name unresolved.","row 239 (junk_deletion_gate.py file rename): CONFLICT — 3 different new module names across rows 239/148/242 (content_integrity_deletion_gate vs nonsource_deletion_gate vs off_topic_deletion_gate); also logger-name-string coupling. Unresolved canonical name.","row 240 (is_row_content_junk -> is_row_content_integrity_violation): CONFLICT with row 238 (same function, different target name). Unresolved.","row 242 (junk_deletion_gate import in multi_section_generator): CONFLICT — part of the 3-way module-name disagreement (rows 239/148/242). Unresolved.","row 265 (PG_V30_ENABLED via _ENABLED_ENV): env-var control-surface string with monkeypatch setenv/delenv + source-grep asserts + launcher env dicts on the old string 'PG_V30_ENABLED'; the symbol is already _ENABLED_ENV so there is no clearer-symbol opportunity. Keep env string per rule.","row 300 (smart_art_diagrams state field): cross-module TypedDict state-KEY used as literal across graph.py/live_server.py + anti_tunnel_view_test.py:57/77 asserts the exact key name in the state annotations/validator (source-grep style). Alias cannot satisfy the exact-name checks without a human test update.","row 323 ([W4-CANARY] log tag): cross-file independent string literals (tool_tracer.py + 8x access_bypass.py) + test assertion test_mineru_http_client_loop_reset_u8.py:100 'assert any("W4-CANARY" in rec.message)'. Not one symbol; changing the emitted string breaks the exact-substring log assertion.","row 324 (contracts_v3 submodule): module/import-surface rename across 9+ files; worklist itself marks it 'deferred as naming decision in SAFE-batch skip doc'. Import-surface alias is a human naming decision.","row 343 (PG_LETHAL_SEED_K): env-var control-surface string (resolve('PG_LETHAL_SEED_K')) + config_defaults.py:390 dict key 'PG_LETHAL_SEED_K'. Renaming the string breaks the resolve key and the config-defaults contract; keep env/config key per rule."],"collection_after":"16738 tests collected, 11 errors (byte-identical to the SAFE-batch baseline; same 11 error files: test_mesh_e2e, test_demo_smoke, test_auth, test_run_paid_evaluator_scoring, test_clean_state, test_clarification_agent, test_hle_benchmark, test_mesh_api, test_mesh_cli, test_mesh_snapshot, test_perspective_tracking)","aliases_verified":true}. Validation: {"collection_ok":true,"characterization_ok":true,"oracle_matches":true,"oracle_sha":"9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98","aliases_all_resolve":true,"notes":"All 4 gates PASS. Gate1 collection: 16738 collected, 11 errors (byte-identical to SAFE-batch baseline). Gate2 config characterization: 9 passed (test_config_registry.py + test_settings_models.py). Gate3 oracle replay: artifact SHA-256 = 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98, BYTE-IDENTICAL to recorded golden; valid_positive_control=True and valid_negative_control=True (both controls pass). Oracle overlay present at tests/oracle/cassettes/ (acceptance_golden.json, acceptance_llm.jsonl, acceptance_retrieval.jsonl, demo_artifact.jsonl, demo_llm.jsonl). Branch chore/review-readiness-phase2 @ 19e32a0.

Gate4 ALIAS RESOLUTION — every applied alias preserves its old reference (verified live):
1) beat_both_scorer -> shim re-exporting from new module head_to_head_dimension_scorer. Both import spellings resolve (polaris_graph.benchmark.beat_both_scorer AND src.polaris_graph.benchmark.beat_both_scorer); all 6 public names (AggregateScoreboard, QuestionScores, Scoreboard, SystemScores, run_benchmark, score_question) present and are the SAME objects as the new module. report_renderer resolves Scoreboard from the new module. New module file /home/polaris/wt/phase2/src/polaris_graph/benchmark/head_to_head_dimension_scorer.py exists (staged).
2) run_honest_sweep_r3: new symbol _ARTIFACT_KIND_DECLINED; old symbol _ARTIFACT_KIND_REFUSAL still resolves to value 'honest-refusal'; persisted literal preserved as a key in _ARTIFACT_KIND_HEADINGS. Consuming test test_artifact_kind_is_named_for_every_status PASSES.
3) scripts/run_live_honest_cycle.py -> renamed to run_live_verified_cycle.py; old path preserved as re-export shim. Old import scripts.run_live_honest_cycle + attr main_async resolves (identical object to new). Consumer tests/polaris_graph/test_b2_corpus_approval_enforcement.py: 8/8 PASS.
4) migrate_old_runs: old value preserved via LEGACY_MODE_LABEL_LEGACY_ALIAS='Legacy run, pre-honest-rebuild'; old key preserved via MIGRATION_MARKER_LEGACY_ALIAS='_honest_rebuild_migration'; _is_already_migrated() recognizes BOTH old and new marker keys (idempotency intact).
5) synthesizer.py: new descriptive bond keys (claim_evidence_binding, evidence_section_affinity, cross_section_consistency, narrative_flow) written ALONGSIDE preserved old keys (covalent, ionic, disulfide, peptide). Module imports cleanly.
6) tool_tracer.py: new manifest key mineru_degraded added; old key mineru_firing still written. Module imports cleanly.

Pure TEXT-ONLY renames (dashboard_visual_audit.py path, screenshot_all_states.py ui_review.pdf/PDF_OUTPUT, claim_dedup.py docstring, data_analyzer.py 'GEMINI-ARCH 2A' log strings, run_live_verified_cycle run_id prefix) carry NO external old-reference consumer — grep found no runtime assertions on the changed literals (sole 'GEMINI-ARCH 2A' test hit is a module docstring, not an assertion).

IMPORTANT CONTEXT on failures: tests/polaris_graph/test_b11_b20_always_emit_lane_sweep.py shows 11 failed / 134 passed, BUT these are PRE-EXISTING and NOT caused by the alias batch. A clean worktree checked out at HEAD 19e32a0 (before the working-tree alias changes) produces the byte-identical failure set (11 failed / 134 passed; diff of sorted FAILED lists is empty). The alias-consuming test in that file is in the passing group. Import root is src.polaris_graph (canonical); PYTHONPATH=src required so sitecustomize installs the import-root alias. No alias fails to preserve its old reference."}. Gates required: collection unchanged, oracle byte-identical (9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98), and every OLD reference still resolves via its alias.
Ask codex: "Do these aliases preserve EVERY old reference (env-var strings, monkeypatch targets, string dict keys, re-exports) so no operator setting or persisted contract breaks? Is it score-safe (collection + oracle unchanged)? Any alias that is cosmetic-only and doesn't actually preserve the old reference? End with: ALIASES-SAFE or ALIASES-REVISE."
Return verdict + points.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll gate this NEEDS-ALIAS rename batch through CODEX. Let me write the gate prompt with the evidence embedded inline.

input.content:
You are gating a batch of "backward-compat alias" renames. Context: control-surface symbols were renamed while adding backward-compat aliases so that OLD strings, monkeypatch-targets, env-vars, dict-keys, and re-exports still resolve. Your job is to judge whether the aliases actually preserve every old reference and whether the change is score-safe.

THE RULE BEING FOLLOWED: env-var control-surface strings, persisted string VALUES, source-grep/AST-name harness targets, and cross-module persisted dict-KEYS are NOT renamed (they are skipped, not aliased) because an alias cannot satisfy an exact source-text grep, an isolated AST-name exec, an exact-value assertion, or an operator's env setting. Only separable symbols (module names, importable functions/classes, symbol-bound values) are renamed WITH an old-name alias re-export/re-bind.

=== WHAT WAS APPLIED (15 rows), with alias-resolution evidence (verified live) ===

1) beat_both_scorer -> new module head_to_head_dimension_scorer. Old module path kept as a shim re-exporting from the new module. BOTH import spellings resolve (polaris_graph.benchmark.beat_both_scorer AND src.polaris_graph.benchmark.beat_both_scorer). All 6 public names (AggregateScoreboard, QuestionScores, Scoreboard, SystemScores, run_benchmark, score_question) present and are the SAME objects as the new module. report_renderer resolves Scoreboard from the new module.

2) run_honest_sweep_r3: new symbol _ARTIFACT_KIND_DECLINED added; OLD symbol _ARTIFACT_KIND_REFUSAL still resolves to value 'honest-refusal'; persisted literal preserved as a key in _ARTIFACT_KIND_HEADINGS. Consuming test test_artifact_kind_is_named_for_every_status PASSES.

3) scripts/run_live_honest_cycle.py -> renamed to run_live_verified_cycle.py; OLD path preserved as a re-export shim. Old import scripts.run_live_honest_cycle + attr main_async resolves (identical object to new). Consumer tests test_b2_corpus_approval_enforcement.py: 8/8 PASS.

4) migrate_old_runs: OLD value preserved via LEGACY_MODE_LABEL_LEGACY_ALIAS='Legacy run, pre-honest-rebuild'; OLD key preserved via MIGRATION_MARKER_LEGACY_ALIAS='_honest_rebuild_migration'; _is_already_migrated() recognizes BOTH old and new marker keys (idempotency intact).

5) synthesizer.py: new descriptive bond keys (claim_evidence_binding, evidence_section_affinity, cross_section_consistency, narrative_flow) written ALONGSIDE preserved OLD keys (covalent, ionic, disulfide, peptide). Module imports cleanly.

6) tool_tracer.py: new manifest key mineru_degraded added; OLD key mineru_firing still written. Module imports cleanly.

Plus TEXT-ONLY renames with NO external old-reference consumer (grep found no runtime assertions on the changed literals): dashboard_visual_audit.py path, screenshot_all_states.py ui_review.pdf/PDF_OUTPUT, claim_dedup.py docstring, data_analyzer.py 'GEMINI-ARCH 2A' log strings, run_live_verified_cycle run_id prefix. The sole 'GEMINI-ARCH 2A' test hit is a module docstring, not an assertion.

=== WHAT WAS SKIPPED (correctly, per the rule) — representative examples ===
- Source-grep harness assertions (anti_tunnel_view_test asserts exact literal 'GEMINI-ARCH: ACCUMULATE structured_data' in graph.py source; no fallback). An alias cannot satisfy a source-text grep.
- Persisted-string VALUES asserted by exact equality (slice_005_beat_both_benchmark, rubber_stamp_suspect, token_explosion) — inline positional strings, no symbol to alias.
- Env-var control-surface strings (PG_V2_ENABLED, PG_JUNK_SOURCE_SCREEN, PG_S15_CORROBORATED_HONEST_LABEL, PG_CONTRADICTION_RENDER_HONEST, PG_V30_ENABLED, PG_LETHAL_SEED_K) — inline os.getenv/os.environ.get with monkeypatch.setenv/delenv and/or source-grep on the OLD string. Keeping the env string per rule.
- AST-name extraction harness (test_wave_b_ws2_paid_slate extracts only nodes whose def-name is in _WANTED old names and execs in isolation; an alias node references a non-extracted new name -> NameError). Rows _WINNER_SLATE_ON_PAID_PATH_ENV, _PAID_PATH_WINNER_FLAGS, winner_slate_on_paid_path_enabled, apply_winner_slate_on_paid_path.
- Cross-module persisted dict-KEY read by exact name across modules + persisted contracts (content_integrity_junk, token_honesty manifest key).
- CONFLICTS where the worklist gives 2-3 different new names for the SAME symbol/file (junk_deletion_gate module: 3 names; is_row_content_junk: 2 names; HonestSweepJobRunner cluster: re-export vs definition disagree). Canonical name unresolved -> skipped rather than guess.
- Persisted verdict-literal VALUES + exact-substring test ('POLARIS BEAT-BOTH' in md).

=== VALIDATION GATES (all PASS) ===
- Gate1 collection: 16738 tests collected, 11 errors — BYTE-IDENTICAL to the SAFE-batch baseline (same 11 error files: test_mesh_e2e, test_demo_smoke, test_auth, test_run_paid_evaluator_scoring, test_clean_state, test_clarification_agent, test_hle_benchmark, test_mesh_api, test_mesh_cli, test_mesh_snapshot, test_perspective_tracking).
- Gate2 config characterization: 9 passed (test_config_registry.py + test_settings_models.py).
- Gate3 oracle replay: artifact SHA-256 = 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98, BYTE-IDENTICAL to recorded golden; valid_positive_control=True and valid_negative_control=True.
- Gate4 alias resolution: every applied alias preserves its old reference (verified live, evidence above).

IMPORTANT on a noisy test file: tests/polaris_graph/test_b11_b20_always_emit_lane_sweep.py shows 11 failed / 134 passed, BUT these are PRE-EXISTING, NOT caused by the alias batch. A clean worktree at HEAD (before the working-tree alias changes) produces the byte-identical failure set (diff of sorted FAILED lists is empty). The alias-consuming test in that file is in the passing group.

=== YOUR TASK ===
Answer precisely:
1. Do these aliases preserve EVERY old reference (env-var strings, monkeypatch targets, string dict keys, re-exports) so no operator setting or persisted contract breaks?
2. Is it score-safe (collection + oracle unchanged)?
3. Is there ANY applied alias that is cosmetic-only and does NOT actually preserve the old reference (i.e., a rename that silently broke an old string/key/import while looking safe)?
4. Is the skip-vs-alias boundary correct — i.e., was anything skipped that could actually have been safely aliased, or anything aliased that should have been skipped?

Reason step by step, then END your answer with exactly one token on its own line: ALIASES-SAFE or ALIASES-REVISE.

content.content: File created successfully at: /tmp/alias_gate.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

input.description: Run codex gate on alias renames
